name: Sigfox LPWAN IoT API
what_it_does: 0G (ultra-narrowband) LPWAN IoT network with REST API — enabling IoT device manufacturers and integrators to connect devices to a global proprietary low-power network for sending small data packets (up to 12 bytes per message), with global coverage in 70+ countries, cloud backend for device and message management, and callback webhooks for real-time data delivery to applications.
use_cases: Receiving Sigfox device messages via API callbacks for IoT sensor data processing
Managing Sigfox device registrations and device types via API for fleet management
Querying Sigfox device message history and geolocation via API for asset tracking
Configuring Sigfox callbacks and API integrations via API for IoT data routing
Managing Sigfox device groups and coverage contracts via API for enterprise IoT deployment
not_for: High-frequency data transmission (Sigfox max 140 messages/day uplink, 4 downlink)
Large payload IoT (max 12 bytes per message — not for streaming data)
Coverage outside Sigfox's 70+ country network (cellular coverage map dependent)
best_when: An IoT manufacturer or enterprise needs ultra-low-power, global connectivity for sending small sensor readings (temperature, GPS, vibration) with multi-year battery life — where Sigfox's simple 0G radio reduces device cost and complexity.
avoid_when: You need frequent messages, large payloads, bidirectional communication, or coverage in areas not served by Sigfox.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with API login + password from Sigfox backend. Per-group or per-user API credentials. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Callback (webhook) for real-time device message delivery to user HTTP endpoint. API keys from Sigfox backend portal. No SDK — REST API only. Callback signature for webhook authentication.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; development kits available ($15-50)
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-device annual connectivity: €10-25/device/year; volume discounts; operator billing
pricing.notes: French company (Labège). Founded 2010 by Ludovic Le Moan and Christophe Fourtet. Raised €300M+. Filed for bankruptcy (January 2022) — acquired by Singapore-based UnaBiz (February 2022). UnaBiz continuing Sigfox network and 0G technology. Network in 70+ countries. 20+ million registered objects. Annual device connectivity pricing model. No monthly platform fee — pay per device per year. Used by: asset tracking, utilities, smart metering, agriculture. Unique 0G radio technology (ultra-narrowband 868/902 MHz). Very long battery life (10+ years) with optimized hardware.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with HTTP Basic auth. Callback (webhook) for real-time device messages. 140 msg/day rate limit is hardware-level constraint. GDPR compliant. Annual contract for device connectivity. Acquired by UnaBiz — product roadmap may change. Very low bandwidth — 12 bytes max per message.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device reads idempotent by device ID. Message history reads idempotent. Callback configuration idempotent. Uplink messages themselves are not retry-guaranteed at network level.
known_agent_gotchas: 140 uplink messages/day/device MAX — cannot poll device frequently
Max 12 bytes per message — payload encoding strategy required
Company acquired by UnaBiz (2022) — evaluate long-term vendor stability
Callback webhook delivery not guaranteed — implement message deduplication
Annual device connectivity contract — upfront commitment required

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. HTTP Basic auth (username + password). Sigfox proprietary radio encryption. Callback signature for webhook verification. French company (now Singaporean) — GDPR awareness. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. Device data routed through Sigfox infrastructure. Company restructuring (acquisition by UnaBiz) — evaluate supply chain.
